key_insight: A Bayesian optimization framework can track a rigidly moving 3D target’s position and orientation from single-incident far-field data by exploiting analytical translation/rotation formulas.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical remote sensing and inverse-scattering tracking when only limited wave measurements are available, with a probabilistic model that supports sequential updates and uncertainty handling.
method: Derive closed-form far-field transformations for target translations and rotations.
Model translational and rotational motion as independent stochastic processes across time steps.
Use Bayesian optimization to jointly estimate location, orientation, and—when unknown—initial shape via a pretrained fully connected neural network.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments validating the framework and states that posterior consistency is established for the probabilistic model.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the precomputed shape dataset and on how well the stochastic motion assumptions match real target dynamics.
paper_title: Bayesian optimization approach for tracking a moving target from far-field data in three dimensions
